items.summary: Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee raised concerns over rising U.S. services inflation, saying recent data showed price pressures moving in an unfavourable direction. He noted inflation remains sticky despite a stable labour market, reinforcing caution among policymakers as the Federal Reserve continues to assess its rate path amid mixed economic signals.

items.title: Global Market: OECD sees Bank of Japan raising rates to 2% by 2027 amid inflation shift
items.summary: Japan's central bank might increase interest rates to two percent by late 2027. This move signals confidence in Japan's economy moving past decades of low inflation. Stronger wages and consumer demand allow for gradual borrowing cost hikes. The Bank of Japan is also expected to reduce bond purchases. This shift marks a significant economic transition for the nation.

items.title: Nifty slips for 4th day; Crude above $100, Rupee at 95.63 weigh on markets
items.summary: The rupee has touched a fresh low of 95.63 against the dollar, with USD/INR hovering in the 95.6–95.7 range amid elevated crude prices, firm dollar demand, and sustained foreign institutional outflows

items.title: Vodafone Idea shares jump 7% as telco set to consider fundraise on May 16. What lies ahead?
items.summary: Vodafone Idea shares surged 7% to a four-month high after announcing a board meeting on May 16 to consider a fundraise via equity and warrants. This follows a significant reduction in its adjusted gross revenue (AGR) dues by the government, boosting investor confidence.

items.title: Tata Power shares crash 7% after Q4. What are Goldman Sachs and Motilal Oswal saying?
items.summary: Tata Power shares dropped after its Q4 FY26 profit declined. Revenue also saw a year-on-year decrease. However, the company's renewable energy segment and solar manufacturing business showed significant growth. Brokerage firms provided varied outlooks, with some recommending 'Buy' and others 'Sell', citing different growth drivers and risks.

items.title: Sensex rises 50 points, Nifty closes above 23,400 as markets snap 4-session losing streak; metal stocks shine
items.summary: Indian stock markets ended in the green, with Sensex and Nifty snapping a four-session losing streak. This occurred despite the rupee hitting a fresh lifetime low. Metal stocks surged following a government hike in import duties, while IT stocks saw declines. Midcap and smallcap indices also recorded gains.

items.title: ​Technical stock picks! 3 shares that could give up to 15% return this May. Do you own any?
items.summary: ​Following a recent downtrend, the Nifty has seen a rally, with analysts suggesting a buy-on-dips strategy. Three technical stock picks from Axis—KEI Industries, Grasim Industries, and Biocon—are highlighted, each with potential upside returns of up to 15% for May.​

items.title: Hindustan Zinc, Vedanta to NALCO: Metal stocks jump after India raises import duty in gold, silver, other metals
items.summary: Metal stocks surged on May 13, driven by rising gold and silver prices after the government raised import duties on precious metals. Hindustan Zinc led the gains, with Nifty Metal climbing 1.3%. Gold and silver prices hit upper circuits, reflecting strong buying interest.

items.title: Crorepati investors splurge $1 billion to buy these 10 stocks. Should you follow the smart money?
items.summary: Wealthy investors are making significant moves, pouring over $1 billion into ten select stocks. This concentrated buying occurs even as their overall portfolio value has decreased. Data shows these high-net-worth individuals are strategically picking specific retail and engineering companies. While some of these chosen stocks also saw price drops, the investors are doubling down on their bets.

items.title: Copper Climbs Toward Record High as Global Supply Tightens
items.summary: Copper extended gains above $14,000 a ton, inching toward a record high seen earlier this year, as supply risks mount on mine disruptions around the world.

items.title: Dr Reddy's shares in focus after Q4 net profit tumbles 86% YoY; Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs weigh in
items.summary: Dr. Reddy's Laboratories shares: The pharma major posted a sharp 86% YoY drop in Q4FY26 consolidated net profit to Rs 221 crore, compared with Rs 1,587 crore a year ago. Following the weak earnings performance, several brokerages cut their target prices on the stock.

items.title: From India to China, a look at what governments worldwide are doing to shield households from rising energy costs
items.summary: Governments globally are implementing diverse strategies to mitigate soaring energy costs stemming from the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran. Measures include fuel tax adjustments, reserve releases, external financing, subsidies, tax cuts, and energy conservation efforts. Countries are also exploring increased use of domestic resources and alternative energy sources.

items.title: Rising inflation, Hormuz deadlock, Trump-Xi meet: What’s driving global news headlines
items.summary: Inflation is flaring up again as the West Asia war disrupts energy flows and keeps oil above $100 amid a prolonged Strait of Hormuz standoff. The Trump-Xi meeting could reshape trade dynamics, while the 2026 World Cup promises a sizeable global growth boost.

items.title: India's energy illusion: why ambitious targets mean nothing without a coherent plan
items.summary: Soaring crude oil prices triggered by the Israel-US-Iran war are straining India's economy, exposing its heavy dependence on imported fossil fuels and a fragile energy strategy.&nbsp;

items.title: India's travel industry braces for hit from Modi's appeal to avoid foreign trips
items.summary: The travel pullback is unfolding against a backdrop of rising global inflation, with crude oil trading above $100 a barrel after Iran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz choked off a fifth of global supply of oil ​and liquefied natural gas

items.title: India’s gold import duty hike to 15% may remain for a year, says Senco MD Suvankar Sen
items.summary: India's higher import duty on gold and silver could impact jewelry demand for a year, Senco MD Suvankar Sen said. Senco Gold predicts volumes may drop 10-15 percent and consumers may opt for lighter jewelry. This move aims to conserve foreign exchange reserves and protect economic stability amid global uncertainty.

items.title: How the US-Israel conflict with Iran is exposing cracks in the petrodollar system
items.summary: Rising geopolitical tensions, global fragmentation and efforts by emerging economies to diversify away from the US dollar are also accelerating concerns over the long-term stability of the US-centred financial order

items.title: Kalyan Jewellers, Titan, other jewellery stocks crash up to 6% as Centre hikes gold customs duty to 15%
items.summary: Jewellery stocks like Kalyan Jewellers and Titan Company tumbled up to 6% after the government hiked customs duty on gold and silver imports to 15%. This move aims to narrow India's trade deficit and support the rupee, but could dampen demand for precious metals. Organised players may fare better than smaller jewellers.

items.title: Trilegal names Ex-Visa India Head Sandeep Ghosh as first CEO
items.summary: Trilegal, a leading Indian law firm, has appointed Sandeep Ghosh as its first CEO, marking a significant shift towards non-legal leadership. Ghosh, with extensive experience in banking and financial services, will drive strategic priorities and growth. This move signals the firm's commitment to adapting to evolving client expectations and technological advancements in the legal industry.

items.title: Indian Hotels among 5 F&O stocks with a sharp rise in futures open interest
items.summary: Five NSE F&O stocks, Kaynes Technology, Vedanta, Indian Hotels, Wipro and KPIT Technologies, recorded sharp futures open interest gains on May 12, signalling heightened trader participation, fresh positions and derivatives market activity.

items.title: Oil prices ease after rising in three consecutive sessions, Brent still around $107/bbl
items.summary: Crude prices pause after an 8% surge, but the 75-day closure of the Strait of Hormuz keeps oil above $100, squeezing India and forcing tough choices as OMC losses swell.

items.title: India-Japan seek to advance coop in critical minerals, semiconductors & ICT
items.summary: India and Japan have strengthened economic ties. A key dialogue focused on industrial and technological cooperation. Five sectors were prioritised: critical minerals, semiconductors, ICT, clean energy, and pharmaceuticals. Both nations aim to build resilient supply chains. Discussions also covered defense, trade, and energy security.

items.title: Sonata Software among 5 stocks closing below VWAP
items.summary: On May 12, five Nifty500 stocks closed more than 2.5% below their VWAP levels, signalling rising bearish pressure. A close below the volume-weighted average price indicates stocks ended the session weaker than their average traded price, reflecting heightened selling activity, weakening intraday sentiment, and possible short-term downside across select counters.

items.title: Oil India among 3 stocks showing bullish RSI upswing
items.summary: Three stocks from the Nifty500 universe appeared in the RSI Trending Up scan on May 12 after witnessing improving momentum. The RSI indicator crossed above 50 from lower levels, signalling strengthening price action, positive sentiment, and potential short-term buying opportunities for traders tracking bullish technical setups and momentum-driven market trends across sectors.

items.title: India raises gold import duty back to 15%: What’s behind the move
items.summary: India has significantly raised import duties on gold, silver, and platinum. This move aims to safeguard the nation's economy and foreign exchange reserves. The government prioritises essential imports like crude oil and fertilisers. This decision reflects a proactive approach to manage external risks.

items.title: Diaspora dollars: be thankful for non-residents sending money home but let’s not taken these flows for granted
items.summary: With India’s foreign-exchange reserves at $691 billion, we can breathe easy on the external front for now. Credit the Indian diaspora for some of this. But resident citizens, as Prime Minister Narendra Modi has urged, must do their bit as well.

items.title: India’s economic strength defies foreign outflow fears, S&P Says
items.summary: S&P Global Ratings said India is coping with global financial pressures better than headline data indicate, adding that concerns over foreign investment outflows are overstated. The agency said India has enough buffers to manage a wider current-account deficit caused by rising oil prices.

items.title: Buzzing stocks: Jewellers, Texmaco Rail, Interarch, Active Clothing, Choice Intl, Puravankara in focus
items.summary: Jewellery stocks may remain under pressure after the government raised gold and silver import duties to 15%, while companies including Texmaco Rail, Interarch Building Solutions, Alfa Transformers and Puravankara announced major order wins, investments and strategic expansion plans

items.title: Mint Quick Edit | India’s inflation incline could put rate hikes back in RBI’s policy consideration set
items.summary: India’s inflation may still be below the Reserve Bank of India’s 4% target, but is on an upward trajectory. Unless price pressures somehow ease, tighter monetary policy may only be a matter of time.

items.title: India books phosphate fertilizer at 40% above pre-war prices
items.summary: India has contracted diammonium phosphate (DAP) fertilizer at prices nearly 40% higher than pre-war levels as the Middle East conflict disrupts supplies and lifts global prices. Indian Potash Ltd. agreed to buy about 1.35 million tons of DAP for delivery on the west and east coasts at around $930-$935 per ton, exceeding the 1.2 million tons sought in last week’s tender.

items.title: India raises gold, silver import tariffs to 15%; revises customs duty on precious metal findings
items.summary: The Indian Government has raised effective import tariffs on gold and silver to 15%. The move aims to curb bullion imports and strengthen the Indian National Rupee against the US Dollar

items.summary: The government imposed a 10% basic customs duty along with a 5% Agriculture Infrastructure and Development Cess

items.title: Noida airport: Regulator AERA fixes Rs 490 UDF for departing domestic passengers
items.summary: Airports tariff regulator AERA has set the User Development Fee (UDF) for Noida International Airport at Rs 490 for departing domestic and Rs 980 for international passengers for the 2026-27 period. These rates are comparable to national averages and aim to encourage airline expansion. The airport, set to begin operations on June 15, will see gradual UDF increases through 2031.

items.title: Blackstone-backed Ventive doubles down on luxury hospitality with  ₹2,000 cr push
items.summary: With a  ₹2,000 crore expansion, Ventive Hospitality aims to capitalize on luxury travel demand. The firm operates 13 assets and plans to grow by focusing on large luxury projects and acquisitions in key markets like Goa, Sri Lanka and the Maldives.

items.title: Aditya Birla Health bets on wellness incentives to improve claims ratios
items.summary: Aditya Birla Health wants to reward healthier customers, nudge those on the edge to be more consistent, and, lastly, intervene with high-risk customers to help them manage their health.

items.title: A third legal salvo against Tata Trusts ahead of key 16 May meeting
items.summary: A legal notice has been sent to the trustees of Sir Ratan Tata Trust regarding the transfer of shares in Tata Sons from a small trust to Naval Tata, father of Noel Tata and the late Ratan Tata.

items.title: Auto Inc stares at stress in Bharat as signs of slowdown emerge
items.summary: India's largest tractor makers and a motorcycle company anticipate declining sales due to poor monsoon conditions, risking stress in the rural auto industry. Mahindra forecasts 5% growth, while Escorts expects a 2-3% decline.&nbsp;

items.title: Video pitch, assessment, socials scan—hiring goes beyond CVs
items.summary: The CV is clearly losing its position as the main document that decides who moves forward. Hiring teams now lean more on assessments, video introductions and digital footprints to judge skills, communication and workplace fit.

items.title: Actis restarts Nxt-Infra sale process, appoints Ambit
items.summary: Interest in road assets has been buoyant in India lately, driven by the availability of a large number of assets that attract large institutional investors, including pension and sovereign wealth funds.

items.title: India raises gold, silver import duty to 15% to curb imports, support rupee amid West Asia crisis
items.summary: Gold, silver import duty hike: India has significantly raised customs duties on gold, silver, and platinum imports to 15% and 15.4%, respectively. This move aims to conserve foreign exchange reserves and stabilise the external account amidst the West Asia conflict and global economic uncertainty. The government seeks to moderate non-essential imports and support macroeconomic stability.

items.title: Inflation in driver’s seat likely to stall discretionary spend
items.summary: While consumer demand had started improving over the past four months after a prolonged slowdown, rising crude oil prices, supply disruptions and Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s recent call for austerity could threaten to derail that recovery, said executives and analysts. The impact is expected to be sharpest in electronics.

items.title: Containing war impact on growth, current account: PMO stitching up plan to boost foreign fund flow
items.summary: Officials at both the finance ministry and Niti Aayog are identifying economic opportunities presented by the Iran conflict, as well as gauging the impact of the surge in global oil prices on the economy under different price bands. Niti Aayog will likely draw up a report on matter.

items.title: Tata Power Q4 PAT up 8%, revenue down 8%, dividend at ₹2.50 per share
items.summary: Praveer Sinha, CEO and Managing Director, Tata Power, said, “This quarter and year reflect our sustained focus on creating long-term value through disciplined growth, operational excellence and strategic partnerships”

items.title: Bridal jewellery gets a gold rush across India as panic buying over policy curbs and higher duties intensifies
items.summary: Consumers are rushing to buy bridal jewellery across India. This surge follows Prime Minister Narendra Modi's call for austerity measures. Jewellers report a significant increase in sales. Customers fear higher import duties or GST on gold. Many are advancing purchases planned for later in the year. This buying frenzy is driven by uncertainty and cultural significance of gold.

items.title: Outbound travel slows as Indians rethink foreign holidays amid crisis and costs
items.summary: Airlines are experiencing weak forward bookings and muted growth plans due to high airfares and a weaker rupee, following a call to postpone foreign travel. While large-scale cancellations haven't occurred, the focus is shifting towards boosting domestic tourism and its economic benefits.

items.title: GDP data release revised to June 7 each year
items.summary: The statistics ministry has adjusted the release date for provisional GDP estimates and fourth-quarter data to June 7 annually, moving from the last working day of May. This change, driven by data availability delays, will see FY26 provisional GDP and Q4FY26 data released on June 5.

items.title: India launches $1.5 billion maritime insurance pool, issues first covers
items.summary: India has launched its significant Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool. The initiative aims to bolster domestic maritime trade by reducing dependence on foreign insurers. The pool offers a substantial sovereign guarantee, ensuring continuous insurance coverage for vessels. This move provides crucial assurance for India's growing maritime sector. The first insurance covers have now been issued to key entities.

items.title: Hospitals warn Central Government Health Scheme reimbursement cap may hurt cancer treatment
items.summary: A major private hospital group warns of a crisis for advanced cancer patients under the Central Government Health Scheme. A reimbursement cap on life-saving immunotherapy drugs is creating financial strain for hospitals. This policy threatens patient access to essential treatments. The association has identified nine specific drugs that are now out of reach for many beneficiaries.

items.title: Brazil Meat Left Out of EU Suppliers List in Risk to Trade
items.summary: Brazil is absent from a list of countries authorized to supply animal products to the European Union, presenting a trade risk to the world’s largest beef and chicken exporter.

items.title: Indian Banks poised for double-digit corporate credit growth on infra, energy, data centre push
items.summary: Indian banks foresee robust corporate loan growth this fiscal year. Demand is surging from key sectors including renewable energy, infrastructure, and manufacturing. Major lenders like SBI and Bank of Baroda report strong loan pipelines. This expansion signals a positive outlook for business lending across the country. However, banks face liquidity challenges in sustaining this growth.

items.title: Voda Idea may consider raising fresh funds to bolster its books
items.summary: Vodafone Idea's board will meet on May 16 to discuss a significant fundraising plan, aiming to secure fresh capital through equity and warrants. This move is crucial for the debt-laden telco to strengthen its financial position, fund network upgrades, and compete effectively against rivals.

items.title: Power, renewable energy ministries to discuss green power curbs and grid penalties
items.summary: Renewable energy and power ministries will convene Wednesday to address industry concerns regarding the deviation settlement mechanism and curtailment of green power. Stricter grid compliance rules have raised viability issues for renewable energy projects, prompting ongoing discussions and government examination of these critical matters.

items.title: NEET-UG 2026 leak row: NTA faces criticism over repeated exam glitches and mismanagement
items.summary: In a disconcerting turn of events, the National Testing Agency finds itself embroiled in fresh controversy following serious allegations of examination paper leaks. This development echoes past challenges faced in 2024 and multiple blunders in 2026, including disruptive power failures and incorrect answer keys.

items.title: Canara Bank chief says core income to support stability after profit decline on treasury losses
items.summary: Canara Bank saw a 10% dip in net profit last quarter, mainly due to treasury losses. The bank is now prioritizing core income to maintain its net interest margins. Advances are expected to grow between 11-12% this year, while deposit growth is projected at 9-10%. Asset quality remains strong with a low slippage ratio.

items.title: Global headwinds put electric bike companies on price hike path
items.summary: Electric two-wheeler companies are feeling the pinch. Rising raw material prices and supply chain issues are impacting profits. Companies like Bajaj Auto, Hero MotoCorp, and Ather Energy are warning of price increases. Demand for AI infrastructure is also affecting the supply of crucial components. Consumers may see higher prices for electric vehicles soon.

items.title: Govt eyes new employment blueprint after labour code rollout
items.summary: The central government has fully operationalised four new labour codes, consolidating 29 laws into a simplified framework designed to be both pro-worker and business-friendly. These codes aim to foster effective coordination between labour and industry. The ministry also plans a comprehensive employment policy to guide future job generation.

items.title: Retail inflation up a tad on rise in food, bullion prices
items.summary: Retail inflation reached a four-month peak of 3.5% in April. This rise was fueled by increasing food and gold/silver prices. Economists anticipate further inflationary pressures ahead. The Reserve Bank of India is expected to maintain its current policy rate. Inflation is nearing the RBI's target, suggesting a pause in rate hikes for now.

items.title: NEET-UG 2026 cancelled amid paper leak allegations, re-exam likely in June
items.summary: The National Testing Authority has taken decisive action by canceling the NEET-UG 2026 examination that took place on May 3, following serious allegations of paper leaks. This swift decision affects around 23 lakh students and is aimed at safeguarding the credibility of the testing system.

items.title: CEA Nageswaran says West Asia crisis a balance of payments stress test; to impact CAD, inflation & exchange rate
items.summary: Large companies must pay micro and small enterprises on time. This eases their working capital and reduces funding costs. Managing the current account, financing it, and preventing currency depreciation are key macroeconomic goals for FY27. The West Asia crisis presents a balance of payments stress test.

items.title: West Asia crisis a wake-up call for self-reliance: Commerce and industry minister Piyush Goyal
items.summary: The West Asia crisis serves as a crucial reminder for Indian industries to boost self-reliance and efficiency. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal urged businesses to reduce import dependency and focus on indigenous design and manufacturing. He also highlighted the need for faster electric vehicle adoption to cut oil bills.

items.title: Japan's Mitsui OSK Lines, world’s second-largest ship owner by fleet size, eyes shipbuilding in India and investments in terminals & logistics
items.summary: Japan's Mitsui OSK Lines plans to build ships in India. The company is also exploring opportunities for roll-on, roll-off terminals. MOL aims to strengthen inland logistics services. This expansion will support its leadership in shipping automobiles from India. The company sees India as a priority region for growth.

items.title: India launches new IFAD-backed programme to boost rural incomes and climate resilience
items.summary: Aligned with the Viksit Bharat@2047 vision, the strategy focuses on empowering rural communities, scaling proven development models, and strengthening grassroots institutions such as Self-Help Groups and Farmer Producer Organisations

items.title: Delhi High Court seeks Centre’s stand on plea to broadcast FIFA World Cup
items.summary: No broadcaster has acquired the broadcasting rights this year for the FIFA World Cup, one of the most viewed sporting events in the world, the petition says

items.title: India-Oman FTA on track for June 1 start even as deal with Chile hits hurdles, says Commerce Minister
items.summary: The India-Oman Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement was signed in December 2025

items.summary: A low-pressure area persisting over the southwest Bay of Bengal is expected to strengthen over the next 48 hours, potentially aiding the monsoon’s progress

items.summary: It will raise about $3.5 billion from overseas markets ⁠via bilateral and syndicated loans

items.title: US wheat harvest set to hit lowest level since 1972 amid severe Plains drought
items.summary: Wheat output for the 2026/27 season is estimated at 1.561 billion bushels, well below last year’s levels and market expectations.

items.title: Adani Ports to invest $1.36 billion in Europe expansion
items.summary: As ⁠part of the planned expansion, its marine services unit Astra Offshore will develop ​a fleet ‌of 200 vessels with specialised offshore capabilities

items.summary: Indian Potash Ltd agreed to buy the cargoes at prices between $930 and $935 per tonne on a cost-and-freight basis for deliveries to both the west and east coasts

items.title: Box office boom pushes film industry back to theatre-first strategy
items.summary: Indian cinema is returning to a theatrical-first release model. Streaming platforms are reducing spending, and studios are focusing on box office success. Films now arrive on OTT services about eight weeks after their cinema debut. This shift marks a departure from direct-to-digital premieres. Box office collections are rising, while digital licensing demand has weakened.

items.title: CCI clears Adani Power's GVK buy, other deals
items.summary: The Competition Commission of India has approved four business deals, including Adani Power's acquisition of GVK Energy and Canada Pension Plan Investment Board's stake in Ctrl S Datacenters. The regulator also cleared Thriveni Earthmovers' purchase of a stake in Llyods Engineering Works and the merger of three entities into the company.

items.title: PM Internship Scheme gets campus hiring option as govt pushes wider industry participation
items.summary: Great news for aspiring interns: the Ministry of Corporate Affairs has opened the doors for companies to recruit directly from academic institutions through the PM Internship Scheme! With the government subsidizing ninety percent of intern stipends, it’s a win-win situation. Recruiters can engage with potential candidates right on campus, making the hiring process streamlined and efficient.

items.title: The toll of structural adjustments on the global South and a case for accountability
items.summary: In the 1980s, IMF and World Bank put conditions on financing the global South. Decades later, many countries in the regions struggle with weak public health systems and high levels of poverty. The institutions owe reparations for the damage caused, a new paper argues.

items.title: China moves WTO against India over solar cells, modules and tech incentives
items.summary: Beijing has alleged that India’s tariff structure and domestic preference policies discriminate against Chinese products and violate WTO agreements related to trade, subsidies and investment measures.

items.title: New Income Tax law aims to build transparent, tech-driven ecosystem for taxpayers: Official
items.summary: Pune's Principal Chief Commissioner of Income Tax, Vivek Wadekar, announced the new Income Tax Act, 2025, aims to simplify compliance and foster transparency. Launched on April 1, 2026, the act features digital initiatives like the 'Kar Saathi' chatbot and 'Kar Setu' for taxpayer assistance.

items.title: Kharif reset: Farmers may ditch paddy and maize for soybean, pulses amid monsoon fears
items.summary: As the kharif season approaches, farmers are leaning towards cultivating oilseeds like soybean and pulses such as urad and masur, primarily due to worries about erratic monsoon patterns and looming fertilizer shortages. Following a recent surge, maize planting is forecasted to decline, as farmers shift towards crops that are less fertilizer-dependent and lower in risk amid unpredictable weather conditions.

items.title: Groww investors Peak XV, Ribbit and Y Combinator divest stake worth Rs 5,326 crore
items.summary: Despite the stake sale, the fintech company recently reported strong financial performance, with profit after tax more than doubling to Rs 686 crore in the March 2026 quarter, while total income surged 81 per cent year-on-year

items.title: ‘How long can oil companies absorb losses?’: Hardeep Singh Puri on fuel prices
items.summary: Speaking about the potential hike in fuel prices, India's Oil Minister Hardeep Puri reassured the public while acknowledging the pressure on oil companies. The minister highlighted the importance of proactively addressing the economic strain caused by current fuel pricing strategies.

items.title: Global turmoil is India’s 'wake-up call' to cut import dependence: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India's Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal urges domestic industry to embrace self-reliance amid global challenges. He calls for increased productivity and better use of trade agreements. The minister emphasizes reducing import dependence to bolster the economy. He also highlights the potential of electric vehicles and advanced technologies like AI for growth. This presents a significant opportunity for India to emerge stronger.

items.title: China urges WTO to set up panel in case against India's support measures for solar, IT sectors
items.summary: China has asked the World Trade Organization to form a panel. This is due to a dispute with India over support for solar cells, modules, and IT sectors. Bilateral talks failed to find a solution. China claims India's measures discriminate against its goods. This follows a similar request in January regarding auto and battery incentives.

items.title: Policy planners outline India’s economic response after PM’s call for energy discipline
items.summary: At the Confederation of Indian Industry’s annual business summit on Tuesday, petroleum minister Hardeep Singh Puri pointed to the stress on state-owned fuel retailers from the global energy shock, which chief economic adviser V. Anantha Nageswaran said poses a 'live macroeconomic stress test'.

Tata Power in talks with three states for its nuclear power project plan
items.summary: Tata Power is taking significant strides in its pursuit of nuclear energy. Currently, negotiations are underway with three states regarding its upcoming projects. Anticipation builds as a comprehensive project report is slated for release within the next six months. Additionally, Tata Power is teaming up with NPCIL to bring these nuclear initiatives to fruition.

items.title: DFS launches Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool for Indian vessels amid risk to operations on  West Asia crisis
IDFC First Bank fraud case: ED arrests 2 former employees in Rs 645 crore embezzlement probe
items.summary: Chandigarh has witnessed a major development as the Enforcement Directorate has apprehended two ex-IDFC First Bank staff amidst a sweeping inquiry into money laundering activities. The individuals are under scrutiny for allegedly embezzling a colossal Rs 590 crore from the Haryana government's coffers, reportedly utilizing ghost companies to make the money disappear.

items.title: Kumaraswamy announces fresh funds to set up 1,243 EV chargers in Karnataka
items.summary: To date, proposals worth ₹503.86 crore have been approved, to facilitate the installation of 4,874 EV chargers in various States through Central Public Sector Enterprises (CPSEs) such as HPCL, IOCL, and BPCL

items.summary: WPP Media India declared brands face "permanent disruption" due to AI, quick commerce, and evolving consumer behavior, reshaping marketing and storytelling. At its BREW 2026 event, leaders discussed AI-led innovation and the need to rethink engagement in a rapidly changing media landscape.

items.summary: "In large parts of Africa, it is likely that technology could fundamentally disrupt this pattern. Research based on World Bank data has predicted that the proportion of jobs threatened in India by automation is 69 percent, in China it is 77 percent and in Ethiopia, the percentage of jobs threatened by automation is 85 percent," he said.
